description:
You are a geography teacher preparing a digital field trip map of high-elevation stratovolcanoes for your class. The Sugar Learning Platform desktop is active. 

Your task is to process a global volcanism dataset and convert it into a valid KML mapping file. 

The raw data is located at: `/home/ga/Documents/volcanoes.csv`
It has the following CSV headers: `Volcano Name,Country,Type,Latitude,Longitude,Elevation`

Using the Terminal activity or Pippy (Python IDE), write a script to:
1. Read the CSV file.
2. Filter the data to include ONLY volcanoes where:
   - `Type` contains the word `Stratovolcano` (case-insensitive)
   - `Elevation` is strictly greater than `3000` meters.
3. Generate an output file at `/home/ga/Documents/high_stratovolcanoes.kml`.

The output MUST be a valid XML/KML file with this exact structure for each matching volcano:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<kml xmlns="http://www.opengis.net/kml/2.2">
  <Document>
    <name>High Elevation Stratovolcanoes</name>
    <Placemark>
      <name>Volcano Name</name>
      <description>Country: X, Elevation: Y m</description>
      <Point>
        <coordinates>longitude,latitude</coordinates>
      </Point>
    </Placemark>
  </Document>
</kml>
```

The coordinates must be in `longitude,latitude` order (KML standard). Each qualifying volcano should appear as a separate `<Placemark>` element.
